BY FEB 24, 2017, PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE VERIFIED, CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE) AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION (SET ONE) WITHOUT OBJECTIONS EXCEPT PRIVILEGE, AND PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS. PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE A SUFFICIENTLY DETAILED LOG FOR ANY DOCUMENTS WITHHELD ON PRIVILEGE GROUNDS. RESPONSES SHALL BE SERVED BY EMAIL AND REGULAR MAIL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR JUN-11-2018 - APPEARANCES AT TRIAL CALL BY PLAINTIFF STEFANIE MORE, SELF-REPRESENTED LITIGANT AND LAURIE SHERWOOD, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT DATAM SF, LLC DBA HOTEL NIKKO SAN FRANCISCO. PLAINTIFF ORALLY REQUESTED A CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE DUE TO HER ILLNESS/HOSPITALIZATION WITH DOCTOR'S DOCUMENTATION PROVIDED. THE COURT FOUND GOOD CAUSE BASED ONLY ON PLAINTIFF'S ILLNESS/MEDICAL CONDITION AND GRANTED THE CONTINUANCE REQUEST. THE TRIAL DATE IS CONTINUED TO JUL-09-2018 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. ANY FURTHER CONTINUANCE DUE TO A MEDICAL CONDITION WILL REQUIRE A DOCTOR'S LETTER/NOTE, AND IF THERE IS ANY SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY, NEW COUNSEL SHALL BE READY TO PROCEED WITH TRIAL AS COUNSEL'S SUBSTITUTING IN WILL NOT BE THE BASIS FOR A CONTINUANCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; MATTER NOT REPORTED (206/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kevin M. Sullivan of the law offices of Kevin Michael Sullivan (415-434-1184) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Stephanie J. Rothberg of the law offices of Walsworth WFBM LLP (415-544-7139) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT DATAM SF LLC, DBA HOTEL NIKKO SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT of Plaintiff's More cause of action for premises liability is DENIED. "[I]f reasonable minds can differ on the question it is one of fact, and that it is only when reasonable minds must come to the conclusion that the defect is so trivial that a reasonable inspection would not have disclosed it, that the question becomes one of law." Fielder v. City of Glendale (1977) 71 Cal.App.3d 719, 731. Evidence of the non-moving party is liberally construed, while that of the moving party is strictly construed. Stationers Corp. v. Dun & Bradstreet, Inc. (1965) 62 Cal.2d 412. The evidence is conflicting as to the size of the depression in the sidewalk. Given this state of the evidence, the Court is unable to conclude that the defect is trivial as a matter of law. A triable issue of material fact as to the dangerousness of the defect is presented, which is a question of fact for the jury to decide.

The Court overrules and sustains disputed facts and evidentiary objections as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312 and CCP 437(c)(g).

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)
